web_text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, to the saints who are at Ephesus, and the faithful in Christ Jesus: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places in Christ; even as he chose us in him before the foundation of the world, that we would be holy and without defect before him in love;
slang_text: It's Paul here, an apostle of Christ Jesus, 'cause that's what God wanted — writin' to the saints out in Ephesus, the faithful crew in Christ Jesus. Grace and peace to you, straight from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Righteous praise to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who's blessed us with every single spiritual blessing up in the heavenly places, all through Christ — He picked us out in Him before the world even got built, so we could be holy and totally spotless in front of Him, wrapped up in love;

web_text: having predestined us for adoption as children through Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his desire, to the praise of the glory of his grace, by which he freely gave us favor in the Beloved, in whom we have our redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of his grace, which he made to abound toward us in all wisdom and prudence,
slang_text: He already had it planned out to adopt us as His own kids through Jesus Christ, 'cause that's just what He was stoked to do, so His grace could get praised for the glory it is — grace He handed us for free through the Beloved One, in whom we got our redemption through His blood, forgiveness for all our screwups, all accordin' to the riches of His grace, which He poured out on us like crazy, loaded with wisdom and smarts,

web_text: making known to us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure which he purposed in him to an administration of the fullness of the times, to sum up all things in Christ, the things in the heavens, and the things on the earth, in him; in whom also we were assigned an inheritance, having been foreordained according to the purpose of him who does all things after the counsel of his will; to the end that we should be to the praise of his glory, we who had before hoped in Christ.
slang_text: He let us in on the mystery of His will, accordin' to what He was already plannin' to do in Christ once the timin' was right, wrappin' everything up together in Christ — everything up in the heavens, everything down here on earth, all in Him. We got handed an inheritance in Him too, already lined up ahead of time by the One who runs everything exactly the way He decided — all so we'd end up bringin' praise to His glory, us who put our hope in Christ first.

web_text: In him you also, having heard the word of the truth, the Good News of your salvation—in whom, having also believed, you were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit, who is a pledge of our inheritance, to the redemption of God’s own possession, to the praise of his glory. For this cause I also, having heard of the faith in the Lord Jesus which is among you, and the love which you have toward all the saints, don’t cease to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers,
slang_text: And in Him, you guys too — once you heard the truth, the Good News about your own salvation, and put your faith in it — you got sealed up with the Holy Spirit He promised, a down payment on our inheritance, till God's own crew gets fully redeemed, all to the praise of His glory. That's why I never quit givin' thanks for you, once I heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all the saints, always mentionin' you in my prayers,

web_text: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give to you a spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him; having the eyes of your hearts enlightened, that you may know what is the hope of his calling, and what are the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints, and what is the exceeding greatness of his power toward us who believe, according to that working of the strength of his might which he worked in Christ, when he raised him from the dead, and made him to sit at his right hand in the heavenly places,
slang_text: so the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of all glory, would hand you a spirit of wisdom and revelation, knowin' Him better; with the eyes of your hearts lit up bright, so you'd get what the hope of His callin' really means, and how loaded with glory His inheritance is among the saints, and how off-the-charts epic His power is for us who believe — same power He cranked up in Christ when He raised Him from the dead and sat Him down at His right hand, way up in the heavenly places,

web_text: far above all rule, and authority, and power, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this age, but also in that which is to come. He put all things in subjection under his feet, and gave him to be head over all things for the assembly, which is his body, the fullness of him who fills all in all.
slang_text: way, way above every ruler, every authority, every power, every throne, and every name you could ever name, not just in this age but in whatever's comin' next too. He put everything down under Christ's feet, and made Him head honcho over everything, for the church — which is His body, totally loaded up with the One who fills up everything, everywhere.

web_text: You were made alive when you were dead in transgressions and sins, in which you once walked according to the course of this world, according to the prince of the power of the air, the spirit who now works in the children of disobedience; among whom we also all once lived in the lusts of our flesh, doing the desires of the flesh and of the mind, and were by nature children of wrath, even as the rest. But God, being rich in mercy, for his great love with which he loved us,
slang_text: You got made alive when you were dead as a doornail in your screwups and sins — the same sins you used to walk around in, chasin' this world's bogus program, followin' whatever that prince of the power of the air, that spirit still workin' in the disobedient crowd, was pushin'. We all used to be right there with 'em, chasin' our own fleshly cravings, doin' whatever our flesh and our minds wanted, born straight-up destined for God's wrath, just like everybody else. But God, loaded with mercy, 'cause of the massive love He had for us,

web_text: even when we were dead through our trespasses, made us alive together with Christ (by grace you have been saved), and raised us up with him, and made us to sit with him in the heavenly places in Christ Jesus, that in the ages to come he might show the exceeding riches of his grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus; for by grace you have been saved through faith, and that not of yourselves; it is the gift of God,
slang_text: even when we were dead in our screwups, made us alive right along with Christ (that's grace savin' you, no less), and raised us up with Him, and sat us right down with Him up in the heavenly places, in Christ Jesus — all so that in the ages still comin' He could show off just how ridiculously rich His grace and kindness are toward us in Christ Jesus. 'Cause it's grace that saved you, through faith, and that ain't somethin' you cooked up yourselves — it's a gift from God, straight up,

web_text: not of works, that no one would boast. For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared before that we would walk in them. Therefore remember that once you, the Gentiles in the flesh, who are called “uncircumcision” by that which is called “circumcision”, (in the flesh, made by hands); that you were at that time separate from Christ, alienated from the commonwealth of Israel, and strangers from the covenants of the promise, having no hope and without God in the world.
slang_text: not somethin' you earned by workin' for it, so nobody gets to brag. 'Cause we're His own handiwork, built in Christ Jesus for good deeds that God already lined up ahead of time for us to walk in. So remember, back in the day, you Gentiles in the flesh, called "uncircumcised" by the crowd who called themselves "circumcised" (a hand-cut thing in the flesh, nothin' more) — back then you were cut off from Christ, locked out of Israel's whole deal, total strangers to the promised covenants, with no hope and no God in this world.

web_text: But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far off are made near in the blood of Christ. For he is our peace, who made both one, and broke down the middle wall of partition, having abolished in his flesh the hostility, the law of commandments contained in ordinances, that he might create in himself one new man of the two, making peace; and might reconcile them both in one body to God through the cross, having killed the hostility thereby.
slang_text: But now, in Christ Jesus, you who used to be way out in left field got brought up close through Christ's own blood. 'Cause He IS our peace, the one who took both sides and made 'em one, smashin' down the wall that used to split 'em apart, wipin' out the hostility in His own flesh — the whole rulebook of commandments and regulations — so He could build one brand-new humanity outta the two, makin' peace happen; and patch both sides up with God in one single body, through the cross, killin' off that hostility for good right there.

web_text: He came and preached peace to you who were far off and to those who were near. For through him we both have our access in one Spirit to the Father. So then you are no longer strangers and foreigners, but you are fellow citizens with the saints, and of the household of God, being built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Christ Jesus himself being the chief cornerstone;
slang_text: He came and preached peace to you guys way out there and to the ones close by too. 'Cause through Him, both sides got the same access to the Father, through one Spirit. So you're not strangers and outsiders anymore — you're fellow citizens with the saints, part of God's own household, built up on the foundation the apostles and prophets laid down, with Christ Jesus Himself as the main cornerstone holdin' the whole thing together;

web_text: in whom the whole building, fitted together, grows into a holy temple in the Lord; in whom you also are built together for a habitation of God in the Spirit.
slang_text: in whom the whole buildin', fitted together piece by piece, keeps growin' into a holy temple in the Lord; and in Him you guys are gettin' built up together too, as a place where God can live, through the Spirit.

web_text: For this cause I, Paul, am the prisoner of Christ Jesus on behalf of you Gentiles, if it is so that you have heard of the administration of that grace of God which was given me toward you; how that by revelation the mystery was made known to me, as I wrote before in few words, by which, when you read, you can perceive my understanding in the mystery of Christ;
slang_text: So that's why I, Paul, am locked up as Christ Jesus' own prisoner, on behalf of you Gentiles — assumin' you already heard about the job God's grace handed me, workin' on your behalf; how the mystery got shown to me through a revelation, like I already wrote you briefly, and readin' that, you can see for yourselves I got a handle on the mystery of Christ;

web_text: which in other generations was not made known to the children of men, as it has now been revealed to his holy apostles and prophets in the Spirit; that the Gentiles are fellow heirs, and fellow members of the body, and fellow partakers of his promise in Christ Jesus through the Good News, of which I was made a servant, according to the gift of that grace of God which was given me according to the working of his power. To me, the very least of all saints, was this grace given, to preach to the Gentiles the unsearchable riches of Christ,
slang_text: which never got shown to earlier generations, but now it's finally been revealed to His holy apostles and prophets through the Spirit — that the Gentiles are fellow heirs, fellow members of the body, fellow partners in His promise, in Christ Jesus, through the Good News. I got made a servant of that Good News, thanks to the gift of God's grace handed to me, workin' through His power. Even though I'm the least of all the saints, way down at the bottom, I still got handed this grace — to preach to the Gentiles the unsearchable riches of Christ,

web_text: and to make all men see what is the administration of the mystery which for ages has been hidden in God, who created all things through Jesus Christ; to the intent that now through the assembly the manifold wisdom of God might be made known to the principalities and the powers in the heavenly places, according to the eternal purpose which he purposed in Christ Jesus our Lord; in whom we have boldness and access in confidence through our faith in him.
slang_text: and to make everybody see how this mystery's supposed to work, the one that's been hidden away in God for ages, God who made everything through Jesus Christ; so that now, through the church, God's crazy-layered wisdom could get shown off to the rulers and powers up in the heavenly places, matchin' up with the eternal plan He already had set in Christ Jesus our Lord; in whom we've got boldness and access, totally confident, through our faith in Him.

web_text: Therefore I ask that you may not lose heart at my troubles for you, which are your glory. For this cause, I bow my knees to the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, from whom every family in heaven and on earth is named, that he would grant you, according to the riches of his glory, that you may be strengthened with power through his Spirit in the inward man;
slang_text: So I'm askin' you not to lose heart over the rough stuff I'm goin' through for your sake — that's actually your glory, believe it or not. That's why I get down on my knees to the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the one every family in heaven and on earth gets its name from, askin' Him to grant you, out of the riches of His glory, that you'd get pumped up strong on the inside, through His Spirit;

web_text: that Christ may dwell in your hearts through faith; to the end that you, being rooted and grounded in love, may be strengthened to comprehend with all the saints what is the width and length and height and depth, and to know Christ’s love which surpasses knowledge, that you may be filled with all the fullness of God. Now to him who is able to do exceedingly abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that works in us,
slang_text: so Christ can move right into your hearts through faith; so that, rooted and grounded deep in love, you'd have the strength to grasp, along with all the saints, just how wide and long and high and deep this thing is, and to know Christ's love that blows past anything you could ever figure out, so you'd get filled up with all of God's own fullness. Now, to the One who's able to pull off way, way more than anything we could ever ask or even dream up, accordin' to the power already workin' inside us,

web_text: to him be the glory in the assembly and in Christ Jesus to all generations forever and ever. Amen.
slang_text: to Him be the glory, in the church and in Christ Jesus, to every generation, forever and ever. Amen.

web_text: I therefore, the prisoner in the Lord, beg you to walk worthily of the calling with which you were called, with all lowliness and humility, with patience, bearing with one another in love; being eager to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace. There is one body, and one Spirit, even as you also were called in one hope of your calling;
slang_text: So me, a prisoner for the Lord, I'm beggin' you to walk in a way that's worthy of the callin' you got called with — stayin' lowly and humble, patient, puttin' up with each other in love; fired up to keep the Spirit's unity locked in with the bond of peace. There's one body, one Spirit, same as you got called to one hope with your callin';

web_text: one Lord, one faith, one baptism, one God and Father of all, who is over all, and through all, and in us all. But to each one of us was the grace given according to the measure of the gift of Christ. Therefore he says, “When he ascended on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts to men.”
slang_text: one Lord, one faith, one baptism, one God and Father over everybody, who's above it all, workin' through it all, livin' in all of us. But each one of us got handed grace, sized up to match the gift Christ measured out. That's why it says, "When He rose up on high, He took the captives captive, and handed out gifts to people."

web_text: Now this, “He ascended”, what is it but that he also first descended into the lower parts of the earth? He who descended is the one who also ascended far above all the heavens, that he might fill all things. He gave some to be apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, shepherds and teachers; for the perfecting of the saints, to the work of serving, to the building up of the body of Christ;
slang_text: Now that "He ascended" bit — what's that mean except that He also went down first, down to the lowest parts of the earth? The one who went down is the same one who went up, way above all the heavens, so He could fill up everything. He handed out some to be apostles, some prophets, some evangelists, some pastors and teachers — all so the saints could get fully equipped, so the work could get done, so Christ's body could get built up;

web_text: until we all attain to the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to a full grown man, to the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ; that we may no longer be children, tossed back and forth and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the trickery of men, in craftiness, after the wiles of error; but speaking truth in love, we may grow up in all things into him, who is the head, Christ; from whom all the body, being fitted and knit together through that which every joint supplies, according to the working in measure of each individual part, makes the body increase to the building up of itself in love.
slang_text: till we all reach the unity of faith and the knowledge of the Son of God, all the way to full-grown adults, matchin' up with the measure of Christ's own full stature; so we're not little kids anymore, tossed back and forth, blown around by every trendy new teachin' outta some slick con artist's schemin', chasin' bogus errors. Instead, speakin' the truth in love, we grow up in every way into Him, who's the head — Christ. From Him, the whole body, fitted and knit together tight, every joint doin' its part, every single member workin' the way it's supposed to, makes the whole body grow, buildin' itself up in love.

web_text: This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that you no longer walk as the rest of the Gentiles also walk, in the futility of their mind, being darkened in their understanding, alienated from the life of God, because of the ignorance that is in them, because of the hardening of their hearts; who having become callous gave themselves up to lust, to work all uncleanness with greediness. But you did not learn Christ that way;
slang_text: So here's what I'm sayin', testifyin' to it in the Lord: don't walk around like the rest of the Gentiles do no more, stuck in the pointlessness of their thinkin', their understandin' all darkened up, cut off from God's own life 'cause of the ignorance eatin' at 'em, 'cause their hearts turned rock hard. They got so numb they handed themselves over to lust, chasin' every grody thing they could get their hands on, greedy for more. But that's not how you learned about Christ;

web_text: if indeed you heard him, and were taught in him, even as truth is in Jesus: that you put away, as concerning your former way of life, the old man, that grows corrupt after the lusts of deceit; and that you be renewed in the spirit of your mind, and put on the new man, who in the likeness of God has been created in righteousness and holiness of truth.
slang_text: since you actually heard about Him and got taught by Him, the truth as it really is in Jesus — that you'd ditch your old self, that old way of livin' that's gone rotten chasin' deceitful cravings; and get made new in the spirit of your mind, and suit up in your new self, made in God's own likeness, built up in righteousness and holiness that's the real deal.

web_text: Therefore putting away falsehood, speak truth each one with his neighbor. For we are members of one another. “Be angry, and don’t sin.” Don’t let the sun go down on your wrath, and don’t give place to the devil. Let him who stole steal no more; but rather let him labor, producing with his hands something that is good, that he may have something to give to him who has need.
slang_text: So drop all the lyin' and speak the truth, each one of you, to your neighbor. 'Cause we're all part of each other. "Get angry, but don't sin over it." Don't let the sun go down while you're still steamin', and don't hand the devil an open door. Whoever used to steal, quit stealin' — go work instead, makin' somethin' good with your own two hands, so you got somethin' to give to whoever's in need.

web_text: Let no corrupt speech proceed out of your mouth, but only what is good for building others up as the need may be, that it may give grace to those who hear. Don’t grieve the Holy Spirit of God, in whom you were sealed for the day of redemption. Let all bitterness, wrath, anger, outcry, and slander, be put away from you, with all malice. And be kind to one another, tender hearted, forgiving each other, just as God also in Christ forgave you.
slang_text: Don't let no grody talk come outta your mouth, only stuff that's good for buildin' folks up wherever they're needin' it, so it hands grace to whoever's listenin'. Don't bum out the Holy Spirit of God, the one who sealed you for the day of redemption. Ditch all the bitterness, rage, anger, yellin', trash-talkin', and every bit of malice you got. And be kind to each other, soft-hearted, forgivin' one another, just like God forgave you through Christ.

web_text: Be therefore imitators of God, as beloved children. Walk in love, even as Christ also loved you, and gave himself up for us, an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweet-smelling fragrance. But sexual immorality, and all uncleanness, or covetousness, let it not even be mentioned among you, as becomes saints; nor filthiness, nor foolish talking, nor jesting, which are not appropriate; but rather giving of thanks.
slang_text: So copy God, like His own beloved kids would. Walk around in love, same as Christ loved you and handed Himself over for us, an offerin' and sacrifice to God, smellin' like the sweetest perfume. But sexual immorality, and every grody thing, or greed — don't even let that stuff get mentioned among you, 'cause that's how saints oughta be; no filthy talk, no dumb jokes, no crude jestin', none of that's appropriate — give thanks instead.

web_text: Know this for sure, that no sexually immoral person, nor unclean person, nor covetous man, who is an idolater, has any inheritance in the Kingdom of Christ and God. Let no one deceive you with empty words. For because of these things, the wrath of God comes on the children of disobedience. Therefore don’t be partakers with them. For you were once darkness, but are now light in the Lord. Walk as children of light,
slang_text: Know this for sure: no sexually immoral person, no grody person, no greedy person (which is basically idol worship) gets any inheritance in the Kingdom of Christ and God. Don't let nobody con you with empty talk — 'cause it's exactly this stuff that brings God's wrath down on the disobedient crowd. So don't go teamin' up with 'em. You used to be darkness, but now you're light in the Lord. So walk like kids of the light,

web_text: for the fruit of the Spirit is in all goodness and righteousness and truth, proving what is well pleasing to the Lord. Have no fellowship with the unfruitful deeds of darkness, but rather even reprove them. For the things which are done by them in secret, it is a shame even to speak of.
slang_text: 'cause the Spirit's fruit is loaded with goodness, righteousness, and truth, provin' out what actually pleases the Lord. Don't go hangin' out with darkness' bogus, empty deeds — call 'em out instead. The stuff they pull off in secret is so shameful it's embarrassin' just to talk about it.

web_text: But all things, when they are reproved, are revealed by the light, for everything that reveals is light. Therefore he says, “Awake, you who sleep, and arise from the dead, and Christ will shine on you.” Therefore watch carefully how you walk, not as unwise, but as wise; redeeming the time, because the days are evil.
slang_text: But everything, once it gets called out, gets shown up plain by the light, 'cause whatever shows stuff up is light itself. That's why it says, "Wake up, you sleepyhead, rise up from the dead, and Christ is gonna shine on you." So watch close how you're walkin', not like some clueless dipstick, but like somebody sharp — makin' the most of your time, 'cause these days are evil.

web_text: Therefore don’t be foolish, but understand what the will of the Lord is. Don’t be drunken with wine, in which is dissipation, but be filled with the Spirit, speaking to one another in psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs; singing, and making melody in your heart to the Lord; giving thanks always concerning all things in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to God, even the Father;
slang_text: So don't be a fool — figure out what the Lord's will actually is. Don't get wasted on wine, that just leads to a wreck — get filled up with the Spirit instead, talkin' to each other in psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, singin' and jammin' out melodies in your heart to the Lord; always givin' thanks for everything, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to God the Father;

web_text: subjecting yourselves to one another in the fear of Christ. Wives, be subject to your own husbands, as to the Lord. For the husband is the head of the wife, as Christ also is the head of the assembly, being himself the savior of the body. But as the assembly is subject to Christ, so let the wives also be to their own husbands in everything.
slang_text: yieldin' to each other outta reverence for Christ. Wives, yield to your own husbands, same as you would to the Lord. 'Cause the husband's the head of the wife, same as Christ is head of the church, and He's the savior of the body Himself. And just like the church yields to Christ, wives oughta yield to their own husbands the same way, in everything.

web_text: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the assembly, and gave himself up for it; that he might sanctify it, having cleansed it by the washing of water with the word, that he might present the assembly to himself gloriously, not having spot or wrinkle or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without defect. Even so husbands also ought to love their own wives as their own bodies. He who loves his own wife loves himself.
slang_text: Husbands, love your wives, same as Christ loved the church and handed Himself over for it; so He could make it holy, cleanin' it up with the washin' of water and the word, so He could present the church to Himself lookin' totally epic, no spot, no wrinkle, none of that grody stuff — holy and totally without a flaw. Same deal, husbands oughta love their own wives like their own bodies. Whoever loves his own wife is really just lovin' himself.

web_text: For no man ever hated his own flesh; but nourishes and cherishes it, even as the Lord also does the assembly; because we are members of his body, of his flesh and bones. “For this cause a man will leave his father and mother, and will be joined to his wife. The two will become one flesh.” This mystery is great, but I speak concerning Christ and of the assembly.
slang_text: 'Cause nobody ever hated their own body — they feed it, they take care of it, same as the Lord does for the church, 'cause we're all part of His body, His own flesh and bones. "That's why a guy's gonna leave his father and mother and get joined to his wife. The two of 'em become one flesh." This mystery's a big deal, but I'm talkin' about Christ and the church here.

web_text: Nevertheless each of you must also love his own wife even as himself; and let the wife see that she respects her husband.
slang_text: Anyway, each one of you dudes better love your own wife like you love yourself, and the wife oughta make sure she respects her husband too.

web_text: Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right. “Honor your father and mother,” which is the first commandment with a promise: “that it may be well with you, and you may live long on the earth.” You fathers, don’t provoke your children to wrath, but nurture them in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.
slang_text: Kids, obey your parents in the Lord, 'cause that's just the right thing to do. "Honor your father and mother" — that's the first commandment that comes with a promise attached: "so things go good for you, and you live a long life here on earth." And you dads, don't wind your kids up till they're fumin' — raise 'em up instead in the Lord's discipline and teachin'.

web_text: Servants, be obedient to those who according to the flesh are your masters, with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as to Christ; not in the way of service only when eyes are on you, as men pleasers; but as servants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart; with good will doing service, as to the Lord, and not to men; knowing that whatever good thing each one does, he will receive the same again from the Lord, whether he is bound or free.
slang_text: Servants, obey your earthly masters with respect and a little fear and tremblin', keepin' your heart focused, like you're servin' Christ Himself — not just puttin' on a show whenever somebody's watchin', tryin' to score points with people. Nah, serve like servants of Christ, doin' God's will straight from the heart; puttin' your whole heart into it, like you're servin' the Lord and not just people; knowin' whatever good thing anybody does, they're gonna get it back from the Lord, whether they're a slave or free.

web_text: You masters, do the same things to them, and give up threatening, knowing that he who is both their Master and yours is in heaven, and there is no partiality with him. Finally, be strong in the Lord, and in the strength of his might. Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil. For our wrestling is not against flesh and blood, but against the principalities, against the powers, against the world’s rulers of the darkness of this age, and against the spiritual forces of wickedness in the heavenly places.
slang_text: And you masters, treat 'em the same way back, and quit with the threats — you both got the same Master, and He's up in heaven, and He doesn't play favorites, not for nobody. Finally, get tough in the Lord, in the strength of His might. Suit up in God's full armor, so you can hold your ground against the devil's slick tricks. 'Cause we're not wrestlin' flesh and blood here — we're up against rulers, powers, the dark overlords runnin' this age, and the wicked spiritual forces up in the heavenly places.

web_text: Therefore put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to withstand in the evil day, and, having done all, to stand. Stand therefore, having the utility belt of truth buckled around your waist, and having put on the breastplate of righteousness, and having fitted your feet with the preparation of the Good News of peace; above all, taking up the shield of faith, with which you will be able to quench all the fiery darts of the evil one.
slang_text: So suit up in God's full armor, so you can stand your ground on the bad day, and after doin' everything, still be standin' at the end. So stand firm — buckle that utility belt of truth right around your waist, strap on the breastplate of righteousness, lace up your boots ready to march out with the Good News of peace; and on top of all that, grab the shield of faith, so you can snuff out every single fiery dart the devil throws at you.

web_text: And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God; with all prayer and requests, praying at all times in the Spirit, and being watchful to this end in all perseverance and requests for all the saints: on my behalf, that utterance may be given to me in opening my mouth, to make known with boldness the mystery of the Good News, for which I am an ambassador in chains; that in it I may speak boldly, as I ought to speak.
slang_text: And grab the helmet of salvation, plus the sword of the Spirit, which is God's word; prayin' every kinda prayer, always in the Spirit, stayin' sharp about it, hangin' tough, prayin' for all the saints — and pray for me too, that I'd get the right words when I open my mouth, so I can boldly lay out the mystery of the Good News, the reason I'm stuck in chains as an ambassador for it; that I'd speak up bold about it, just like I oughta.

web_text: But that you also may know my affairs, how I am doing, Tychicus, the beloved brother and faithful servant in the Lord, will make known to you all things; whom I have sent to you for this very purpose, that you may know our state, and that he may comfort your hearts. Peace be to the brothers, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Grace be with all those who love our Lord Jesus Christ with incorruptible love. Amen.
slang_text: But so you'd know how I'm doin' and what's up with me, Tychicus, our beloved bro and faithful servant in the Lord, is gonna fill you in on everything. I sent him out to you for exactly that reason, so you'd know how we're doin' and so he could put your hearts at ease. Peace to the brothers, and love plus faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Grace be with everybody who loves our Lord Jesus Christ with a love that never fades. Amen.
